title: Talk to Shogouki
wikitext:
[]: Talk to Shogouki             Quote Post by edmundasBakashinji » Mon Mar 22, 2010 6:31 am One day if you find yourself hard to meet/talk to any human being, or experiencing hardships, as a Eva fan, will you ask Chogoki for help? Assume that there is a physical Chogoki besides you. It seems like praying...I don't know.

[]:             Quote Post by master_lloyd » Mon Mar 22, 2010 7:42 am Surely, if there was a giant humanoid war-machine next to me everybody would want to be my friend? OR BE DESTROYED! If nothing else it would be a great conversation starter... "What's that?" "Giant death-robot," "...cool..."

[]:             Quote Post by edmundasBakashinji » Mon Mar 22, 2010 8:26 am Of course I didn't mean the real big one, the model one, or even doll... I suppose many of you may get collect such kind of things...

[]:             Quote Post by Otakon 08 Ikari » Mon Mar 22, 2010 11:14 am sadly, haha all my figures just give me an angry look including the Megazord. I sometimes lay in bed and look up at the different wall scrolls while listening to the life outside my window.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Mar 22, 2010 2:27 pm Is edmundasBakashinji advocating Yui-sama worship? Lately I've been yelling at the TV while playing NGE2 because Eva-01 refuses to go berserk on my many attempts to initiate certain events. So I'm effectively talking to Eva-01 when I hiss, "Mom, how could you let me just die like that? You worthless bitch!!!" I used to write weird little fanfics for myself where I would interview the Evas in person, too. I have no idea what happened to those, and maybe it's for the best.

[]: Re: Talk to Shogouki             Quote Post by supershinjiasukashipper » Mon Mar 22, 2010 5:48 pm Personally, I prefer god. On another topic, Eva must be gods all time favorite out of anime. It is very soulful.

[]:             Quote Post by Uriel Septim VII » Mon Mar 22, 2010 6:01 pm Talking to an EVA is like talking to an autistic savant with a penchant for making animal sounds.

[]:             Quote Post by supershinjiasukashipper » Mon Mar 22, 2010 6:05 pm Uriel Septim VII wrote: Talking to an EVA is like talking to an autistic savant with a penchant for making animal sounds.

[]:             Quote Post by edmundasBakashinji » Mon Mar 22, 2010 9:45 pm Reichu wrote: Is edmundasBakashinji advocating Yui-sama worship? Lately I've been yelling at the TV while playing NGE2 because Eva-01 refuses to go berserk on my many attempts to initiate certain events. So I'm effectively talking to Eva-01 when I hiss, "Mom, how could you let me just die like that? You worthless bitch!!!" I used to write weird little fanfics for myself where I would interview the Evas in person, too. I have no idea what happened to those, and maybe it's for the best.

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Mon Mar 22, 2010 9:54 pm I would ask the Eva who its favorite band is. If it answered anything other than 200 Proof, it would mean death to the Eva.

[]:             Quote Post by Zephros » Mon Mar 22, 2010 10:36 pm I'm seated in my chair, looking dully at the window while the evageeks forum is up on my laptop. There are birds flying to a feeder, eating the seeds my wife left for them. We had a fight in the last hour, one where I cursed her and she walked out the door, leaving me to feel hollow and numb. I wish to connect with someone who would understand. My sister's phone is lost and I only got a hold of my brother's snarky voicemail. My parents...I'll not drudge that up here. I return my attention to screen and contemplate writing to the forum of my problem, hoping they will empathize and give me motivation and encouragement. Yet, I don't know them. Awesome as some appear, they're strangers in a strange forum I'm still testing the waters of. I remove my hand from my mouse and wait for a second, breathless with hope that my wife will return, and we can talk. Interact, allowing me to leave my foolishness behind us. The only answer to my expectations of her return are the tweets from the birds outside. I hang my head and sigh. It is then that a light manifests nest to me, slowly fading to reveal the human sized Shogoki. I smile for I think I've got mad from stress. It puts a metallic arm around my shoulders. I breakdown and speak. "I don't know what came over me. I just...lost it! I mean, she accused me of not loving her! How could she doubt me!" Rage comes again, but the hand patting my shoulder calms me. I turn to stare at its rigid face and see my answer laid bare. "You're right, I'm not seeing it from her point of view." The moment is liken to a moment of Eureka. "She's scared is all, scared of losing me." Another moment comes over me. "And I'm scared of losing her." I whisper. I again look to Shogoki as it stands. I offer it my hand and we shake, understanding passing between us. In that moment, I realize that the soul residing within understands my plight and is beseeching me to talk to my wife about my feelings. About the need to communicate. In that moment shared between us I understand her regrets. Of how she wished her and her own husband had discussed their own views, before humanity became a soup. Light again masks the figure and it is gone, like a cool breeze on a spring day. That's what I'd say to Shogoki is I were in such a position.

[]:             Quote Post by felineki » Tue Mar 23, 2010 2:43 am Reichu wrote: I used to write weird little fanfics for myself where I would interview the Evas in person, too. I have no idea what happened to those, and maybe it's for the best.

[]:             Quote Post by Halicat » Tue Mar 23, 2010 10:09 am @Zephros, awesome post. I can relate [Aside from Shogoki though] Erm, I wouldn't talk to her unless she was actually real right there in front of me. If I did anyway that would be idolatry so that's out of the question for me

[]:             Quote Post by Defectron » Tue Mar 23, 2010 11:10 am Of course I didn't mean the real big one, the model one, or even doll... I suppose many of you may get collect such kind of things...

[]:             Quote Post by Sparkie » Sat Apr 03, 2010 11:34 am edmundasBakashinji wrote: Of course I didn't mean the real big one, the model one, or even doll...

[]:             Quote Post by tomrule123 » Sat Apr 03, 2010 1:20 pm If it were Shogouki that I would talk to, I'd say, "How's the weather up there?" (ok, so it's a stale joke, but we need a couple of laughs)

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Sat Apr 03, 2010 6:51 pm What is a Chogoki?

[]:             Quote Post by planet news » Sat Apr 03, 2010 6:58 pm I was wondering this myself. I guess it just means the small figurines.

[]:             Quote Post by Sparkie » Sun Apr 04, 2010 1:43 am Bwaahaha! What's Chogoki? It's either how you spell Spark pays no attention or it's, like, a new dance craze. Sorry all!
